title: podman build --cgroup-parent: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container
body:
This is an old error, one of the `skips` in the buildah-bud-tests-under-podman. I'm reporting now because I'm testing against #10411 (vendor buildah v1.21.0) and it's still not fixed.
```console
$ printf "FROM alpine\nRUN echo hi\n" | ./bin/podman build --cgroup-parent test-cgroup -t foo -
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: RUN echo hi
error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c echo hi]: sd-bus call: Invalid argument
: exit status 1
Error: error building at STEP "RUN echo hi": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

comments.body:
`sd-bus call`

Is this test using systemd cgroups? If so, that cgroup parent is not valid for these test conditions.

Good hint, thanks. It must be (using systemd) because `podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` works fine.

So, what would you recommend for this test? The options are:
* skip the `"bud with --cgroup-parent"` test under podman
* add `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` to all `podman build` invocations in the `test-builda-bud` suite.

There is no option to add `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` only for that one particular test. That would complicate things way too much.

I lean toward skipping the test entirely, because running all tests with `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` does not strike me as representative of a user environment. Any objection?
Another alternative would be to change the cgroup parent to a valid systemd slice (`system.slice` maybe?). Barring that, disabling the test seems reasonable.
Opting to `skip` in latest #9887. Closing this issue. If it's important to test this functionality in podman, please reopen this as a request to add a new podman system test.
